summary: Partially decompiled Peach's float-attack code by adding src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_FloatAttack.c and headers, removing the corresponding ftPe_FloatAttackAir assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s, and splitting nearby Peach code into ftPe_11C1.c. The PR also introduced reusable fighter inline ftCheckThrowB3 and applied it across Donkey, Fox, Luigi, Mario, Marth, and Peach call sites to replace repeated check-and-clear ThrowFlags.b3 patterns.
what_changed: Added ftPe_FloatAttack.c with C implementations for ftPe_8011BE80, ftPe_8011BF34, ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Anim, ftPe_FloatAttackAir_IASA, ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Phys, and ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Coll, while retaining inline assembly under MUST_MATCH/!WIP for functions that were not fully matched.
Deleted the ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Anim, ftPe_FloatAttackAir_IASA, ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Phys, ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Coll, and ftPe_8011C1C0 assembly block from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s.
Added ftPe_11C1.c and ftPe_11C1.h for ftPe_8011C1C0, keeping that function as MWERKS_GEKKO inline assembly in a source file rather than in the large ftPe_Init.s assembly file.
Moved code previously appended to ftPe_FloatFall.c out of that file; ftPe_FloatFall.c was reduced back to its float-fall collision function in the excerpt.
Added ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput(Fighter*) in ftPe_Float.c and exposed it via ftPe_Init.h, replacing the older ftPe_8011BA20-style declaration/name in the header excerpt.
Changed Peach motion variables in types.h from a floatfall int x0 at fp+2340 to a floatattack bool x0 at the same offset, matching the code's use of this flag during float attack.
Added ftCheckThrowB3(Fighter*) to src/melee/ft/inlines.h; it checks fp->x2210_ThrowFlags.b3, clears it, and returns whether it had been set.
Replaced duplicated ThrowFlags.b3 check-clear boilerplate with ftCheckThrowB3 in Donkey, Fox, Luigi, Mario, Marth, and Peach files.
Added prototypes for ft_80095328(HSD_GObj*, bool) and ft_8008D5FC(HSD_GObj*) to ft_0877.h to support the new float-attack C code.
Updated obj_files.mk to compile ftPe_FloatAttack.c.o and ftPe_11C1.c.o before the remaining ftPe_Init.s.o assembly object.
smart_moves: Factored the recurring throw-flag check-and-clear idiom into ftCheckThrowB3, reducing local bool/phi variables and making several unrelated fighter functions simpler while preserving the side effect of clearing b3.
Used a named Peach helper, ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput, for the shared continue-float input condition instead of leaving it as an anonymous or address-based function name.
Split Peach float attack into its own source/header pair rather than continuing to grow ftPe_Init.s or unrelated ftPe_FloatFall.c, improving file ownership around action-specific code.

Kept inline assembly fallbacks under MUST_MATCH/!WIP and MWERKS_GEKKO for code that was not yet cleanly matched, allowing partial decomp progress without pretending every function was fully C-matched.
Updated the motion variable union to reflect actual use at fp+2340 as a floatattack bool flag, helping future code read fp->mv.pe.floatattack.x0 semantically.
decomp_lessons: When the same bit-test-and-clear sequence appears in multiple fighters, an inline helper can improve readability and may also help matching by standardizing the generated pattern.
Partial decomp PRs can still remove large asm chunks by moving not-yet-matched routines into guarded inline assembly inside source files, narrowing the remaining assembly surface.
Use action-specific source files for action callbacks such as Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll; keeping float attack out of float fall and init files makes future symbol ownership easier.

Header renames from address-style placeholders to semantic names should be evidence-backed by usage; here ftPe_8011BA20 became ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput because its body checks left-stick-up or XY-held float continuation input.
Motion variable union names should follow the action that owns the offset, even when the offset is shared with previous placeholder naming.
naming_conventions: Peach action files use ftPe_FloatAttack.c / ftPe_FloatAttack.h for FloatAttack-specific callbacks.
Callbacks follow the fighter/action suffix pattern: ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Anim, ftPe_FloatAttackAir_IASA, ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Phys, ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Coll.
New helper ftPe_Float_CheckContinueInput uses a semantic verb phrase instead of the old address-style ftPe_8011BA20 name.
Unmatched or unknown Peach function ftPe_8011C1C0 remained address-named and was placed in ftPe_11C1.c / ftPe_11C1.h, matching the address-range file naming convention.
The generic inline helper is named ftCheckThrowB3 and lives in ft/inlines.h because it operates on common Fighter throw flags across characters.
Peach motion variable naming was changed to fp->mv.pe.floatattack.x0 with struct ftPe_FloatAttackVars.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed direct assembly for float-attack routines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s once corresponding source objects were added.
Preserved exact assembly for ftPe_8011BE80 and ftPe_FloatAttackAir_IASA under #if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP), with C versions available in the else branch.
Used #ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO inline asm for ftPe_8011C1C0 in ftPe_11C1.c, keeping that function buildable for the matching compiler while isolating it from the old asm file.
Maintained object ordering in obj_files.mk by adding ftPe_FloatAttack.c.o and ftPe_11C1.c.o before the remaining ftPe_Init.s.o.
Reduced temporary local variables in several IASA/Anim functions by replacing manual bool flag patterns with ftCheckThrowB3, a common tactic when compiler phi/local layout is sensitive.
Used small explicit MUST_MATCH stack padding blocks where needed, such as unused u8 arrays in Mario/Marth/Peach functions, to preserve stack layout.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body excerpt contains only an image link, so rationale beyond the title and diff is not available from human text.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully C-match ftPe_FloatAttackAir_IASA or ftPe_8011BE80 after the MUST_MATCH inline asm fallback.
Search for additional repeated x2210_ThrowFlags.b3 check-clear patterns that could use ftCheckThrowB3.
Check later Peach PRs for semantic naming of ftPe_8011C1C0 and the ftPe_11C1 file.
Compare generated assembly for ftPe_FloatAttackAir_Anim using ftCheckThrowB3 to confirm the helper preserved the intended bit-clear pattern.
